clean_code:
# Resample minute data to daily first
minute_daily = minute.resample('1D').agg({'close': 'last', 'volume': 'sum'})
df = daily.merge(minute_daily, left_index=True, right_index=True, how='left')
description: A daily dataset is merged with a minute-frequency dataset on a shared 'date' column.
explanation: Daily data has one row per day. Minute data has hundreds of rows per day. Merging on 'date' without resampling will either silently drop most minute rows (if date is a date not datetime) or explode the daily rows into hundreds of duplicates (if date includes time). Either outcome corrupts the dataset.
suggested_fix: Resample the higher-frequency dataset to match the lower frequency before merging. Be explicit about the aggregation method (last price, sum volume, etc.).

issues.title: Merging datasets with different time frequencies
Merging datasets with different time frequencies
